steps.title: Recognize the structure of the budget inequality
steps.content:
The inequality $11x + 14y \leq 115$ represents Anthony's total spending constraint.

Each term follows the pattern (unit price) × (quantity), where the product equals the total amount spent on that item type.

So each term in the left side must be a total spent on one of the pizza types.

steps.title: Identify what 14y represents
steps.content:
In $14y$, we have:
  • $y$ = the number of large pizzas (given in the problem)
  • $14$ = the cost per large pizza (from the structure of the inequality, this multiplies $y$)
  • Therefore $14y = 14 \times y$ = the total amount spent on large cheese pizzas
That matches choice C — C is correct.
